commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

shockwaves at 5x, and a spin push at 8x. Leans into the #1 user
interaction from telemetry (58 canvas taps across 61 sessions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(cli): transitive type discovery for inline union members (#192)

## Why

When a union type references members that are not explicitly exported as
schema types (e.g., local interfaces used in `type Container = { items:
Array<PartA | PartB> }`), these types were previously either unresolved
or expanded as inline objects with auto-generated names like
`ContainerItemsPartA`. The original type names should be preserved.

## Summary

- Added a **discovery map** (`discoveredTypes`) to
`FieldTypeResolverContext` that records unknown named types encountered
during field resolution
- After the main extraction loop in `type-extractor`, discovered types
are converted to `ExtractedTypeInfo` with their original names via a
transitive while-loop
- The orchestrator augments `knownTypeNames` with discovered types
before resolver extraction, so `inline-union-collector` treats them as
known members (`needsAutoGeneration: false`)
- Added guard in `field-type-resolver` to skip inline object expansion
for types already in the `discoveredTypes` map
- Fixed missing **array-of-union field type replacement** in
`auto-type-generator` (only array-of-inlineEnum was handled)
- Added golden test cases: `inline-union-discovered-basic`,
`inline-union-discovered-array`, `inline-union-discovered-transitive`

### Before
```graphql
type Container { items: [String!]! }  # wrong
type ContainerItemsPartA { value: String! }  # auto-generated name
```

### After
```graphql
type Container { items: [ContainerItems!]! }
type PartA { value: String! }
type PartB { count: Float! }
union ContainerItems = PartA | PartB
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix WASM frontend: add wasm_bindgen(start) entry point to lib.rs

The nix WASM build uses --lib which only builds lib.rs. The Dioxus
launch() call was only in main.rs, so the WASM output was a 13KB
stub with no app code. Adding #[wasm_bindgen(start)] to lib.rs
ensures the app bootstraps when loaded as a WASM module.

commit.message:
add user-provided Groq API key support (#44)

* add user-provided Groq API key support

Adds a new 'groq' provider option letting users enter their own Groq API
key so they get personal rate limits instead of sharing the free-tier quota.

- New GroqProviderSettings type and PROVIDER_CONFIGS entry with suggested models
- Proxy accepts X-Groq-API-Key header; uses it over env key when present
  - Validates gsk_ format before forwarding
  - Never logs the key value; differentiates error messages (user key vs shared)
- createGroqClient() routes through proxy with key in custom header
- formatLLMError() handles groq-specific messages (personal rate limit, bad key)
- Storage persists groq settings (including apiKey) in localStorage
- Connection test validates key against Groq before saving
- LLMSettings UI: shows API key field for groq, pre-fills saved key on re-open,
  validates key on save, shows security disclosure and help section

API keys are NOT included in share URLs (StoredReading doesn't include
LLM settings) so sharing readings is safe regardless of which provider is used.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01D919467rrTUdDXht19ZJ5c

* Improve free-tier rate limit messages with API key guidance

When users hit the shared Groq key's rate/quota limits, suggest they
add their own free Groq API key via Settings → LLM Provider. Updated
messages in the proxy, freeTier quota checks, and client error handler.

Also added a clickable console.groq.com/keys link to the Settings
usage-warning that appears at ≥80% daily quota consumption.

---------

fix: corregir fórmulas, eliminar renta corta, cashflow T0, patrimonio desglose

=== Fórmulas ===
- Corretaje: prorrateo /12 (anual) en vez de /24 (bianual)
- Servicios básicos: eliminados (los paga el arrendatario en renta larga)
- GGCC: verificado como gasto del propietario (sin cambios)

=== Renta corta eliminada ===
- Formulario: sección "Destino del Arriendo" simplificada a solo "Arriendo"
- Motor: toda lógica Airbnb eliminada (tipoRenta siempre "larga")
- Types: campos Airbnb y serviciosBasicos removidos
- API routes: simplificadas

=== Flujo de caja T0 ===
- Entrega inmediata: T0 muestra inversión inicial (pie) como barra roja
- Acumulado empieza en -pie y sube/baja con el flujo mensual
- Verde/blanco: T0 es primera cuota, pre-entrega solo cuotas pie

=== Patrimonio ===
- Gráfico siempre visible (removido filtro horizonYears >= 3)
- Card desglose debajo: pie + plusvalía + amortización + flujo = patrimonio
- Valores se actualizan con el slider de años

=== Sensibilidad client-side ===
- Scoring actualizado a tablas granulares por comuna (consistente con engine)
- Riesgo: base 50, oversupply, ratio egresos/ingresos

- 4-pass route optimizer (loop prune, chain straighten, re-prune, via prune)

- Tile integration updated for new layout coordinates

commit.message:
Replace panicking unwraps with graceful error handling

Audit and fix stability issues that could crash the compositor:

- dispatch_clients/flush_clients: log errors instead of unwrap
- renderer.bind(): skip frame on failure instead of expect
- backend.submit(): log error instead of unwrap
- insert_client: log and reject instead of unwrap
- get_keyboard()/get_pointer(): early return with let-else instead
  of unwrap (in main.rs, handlers.rs, state.rs, input.rs)

Remaining unwraps are all safe: mutex locks (only panic on poisoning),
const array access, and Smithay-internal data access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: AudioRecorder Singleton 세션 가드 강화

- record() 진입 시 isRecording 체크 → 이미 녹음 중이면 즉시 반환 + 경고 로그
  (Practice/Study/Test 간 화면 전환 시 이중 녹음 및 마이크 충돌 방지)
- AtomicInteger 세션 카운터 도입 → 각 record() 호출마다 고유 세션 ID 부여
- while 루프 조건에 activeSessionId == mySessionId 추가

commit.message:
Fix server-side entitlement block rejecting all platform connects

exchangeOAuthTokens was blocking new connections when max_platforms=0:
  allConnections.length (0) >= maxPlatforms (0) → true → 403

This killed the OAuth callback for every user without a paid subscription.
Now only enforces the limit when max_platforms > 0 (real subscription limit).
Same logic already applied to client-side check.

IMPORTANT: This edge function must be redeployed to take effect.

commit.message:
Initial release: Software Intent Runtime (SIR)

Compiler-style architecture that treats LLMs as compilers,
translating human intent through graph IR into software artifacts.

- Core IR: Node/Edge/Snapshot models, NetworkX graph, validator
- Patch system: 5 atomic operations, deterministic engine
- Adapter: Generic 1:1 lowering with path conventions
- Generator: Python code stubs + JSON config
- LLM providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, any OpenAI-compatible
- MCP server: 9 tools for AI tool integration
- CLI: init/compile/snapshot/patch/generate/validate/mcp
- 105 tests, all passing

commit.message:
Add mixed-data training: memory tasks + FineWeb

Pure FineWeb training causes VT to degrade (9→3/10 over 500→3000 steps)
as sidecar gates grow but learn noise. Mixed training interleaves synthetic
AR/VT/Passkey examples (seed=1337, separate from eval seed=42) to give the
sidecar a gradient signal for actual recall.

Results with 10% memory ratio: VT stays in 6-9 range over 2000 steps
instead of collapsing. Non-monotonic VT (8→6→9→6) indicates active
sidecar learning vs pure FineWeb's monotonic decline.

New features:
- build_mixed_dataloader() in src/data/dataloader.py
- --memory_task_ratio flag (default 0.0 = pure FineWeb)
- --freeze_gates_at flag (optional fixed gate value)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix AppleScript: iterate accounts/calendars instead of 'whose'

Calendar.app doesn't support 'whose' filtering on accounts.
Use iteration pattern (same as mail-reader.ts) to find the
target calendar by name.

commit.message:
fix: use struct key for vertex grouping to prevent hash collision artifacts

The previous long-hash key allowed different vertex positions to collide
and merge normals incorrectly, producing rectangular fin artifacts on
barrel-like geometry. Using a proper IEquatable struct ensures equality
checks all three quantized coordinates, not just the hash.
